headlines.text: CENTCOM prepares new strike options for Iran as Trump rejects Hormuz-for-nuke-talks deal
Gas hits $4.30/gal nationally -- up 27 cents in one week -- highest since July 2022
Dow surges 730 pts on Caterpillar blowout; Meta craters 9% on $145B AI capex
Supreme Court guts Voting Rights Act Section 2, setting off redistricting free-for-all
Michael Jackson biopic opens to record $218.8M globally, critics shrug, audiences moonwalk in
USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier heading home from Middle East as war enters stalemate
Israel intercepts Gaza aid flotilla near Crete; 16 killed in Israeli airstrikes on Lebanon
UK PM Starmer accuses Iran of wanting to harm British Jews after London stabbing attack
Louisiana Gov. Landry moves to suspend May primary elections to redraw congressional map
PCE inflation rose 3.5% year-over-year in March; Fed holds rates steady for third time

breaking.text: CENTCOM chief to brief Trump today on new 'short and powerful' Iran strike options as peace talks stall

stories.hed: Trump rejects Iran deal, gets briefed on new strike options as gas prices blow past $4.30
stories.dek: CENTCOM prepared a 'short and powerful' wave of new strikes and Trump is being briefed today, which is a very normal Thursday. Iran's parliament speaker meanwhile trolled Trump's oil-well-explosion prediction by saying 'three days in, no well exploded -- we could livestream it on X.'

stories.hed: UK PM Starmer accuses Iran of directing terror against British Jews after north London stabbing
stories.dek: Two Jewish men were stabbed in the street in north London, and PM Keir Starmer stood at Downing Street to declare Iran wants 'to harm British Jews' and called for stronger powers against hostile state proxies. Meanwhile, Israel intercepted a Gaza aid flotilla near the Greek island of Crete.

stories.hed: Supreme Court guts Voting Rights Act, opening redistricting chaos across the South
stories.dek: The Court tossed Louisiana's congressional map and dramatically narrowed Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act, making it much harder to challenge racially discriminatory maps. Florida and Louisiana governors moved immediately to redraw lines, potentially adding multiple GOP House seats.

stories.hed: Caterpillar pops 10%, Meta tanks 9% -- markets split as earnings season gets weird
stories.dek: The Dow surged 730 points on the back of Caterpillar's blowout quarter and raised guidance, while Meta plunged after hiking AI capex to $125-145B. The S&P 500 gained 0.5% and the Nasdaq barely budged, because that is what markets do when they cannot make up their mind.

stories.hed: Gas was under $3 before this war. Now it's $4.30. Americans are about to get very opinionated about foreign policy.
stories.dek: AAA confirmed the national average jumped 27 cents in a single week to $4.30, up 44% since the Iran war began in late February. Gas prices were below $3 before the first bomb dropped, which means every driver in America is now personally invested in Strait of Hormuz diplomacy whether they wanted to be or not.

markets.context: Caterpillar's blowout earnings and easing oil prices lifted the Dow while Meta's monster AI capex hike dragged tech down, splitting Wall Street neatly in half.

gas.note: Gas was under $3 before the war. Iran really said 'let me help you cut back on driving.'

popCulture.hed: Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael' moonwalks to record $218.8M global opening, biggest music biopic ever
popCulture.dek: Directed by Antoine Fuqua and starring MJ's actual nephew Jaafar Jackson, the film blew past all tracking to open at $97M domestic -- the biggest biopic debut ever, topping Oppenheimer. Critics called it sanitized; audiences reportedly danced in the aisles and did not care.

genZ.definition: A TikTok trend using the audio 'Freakin Out' by Dexter and the Moonrocks where creators montage nostalgic clips targeting a specific generation or niche demographic with the phrase 'Remember who you are, [2000s baby / elder millennial / etc.].' It hit peak velocity this week as Euphoria Season 3 and the Michael biopic sent everyone into a nostalgia spiral simultaneously.
genZ.usage: Remember who you are, person who thought gas would stay under $3 forever.

tldr: The Iran war hit day 62 with no deal in sight, CENTCOM prepping new strike options, and gas prices now at a 4-year high of $4.30 a gallon -- a number that is doing more for antiwar sentiment than any congressional hearing. Meanwhile, the Michael Jackson biopic broke box office records, the Supreme Court kneecapped the Voting Rights Act, and Caterpillar single-handedly saved the Dow.
